intitule: So...that vs Such...that: Fill in the Blanks

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with so or such to make them grammatically correct.

questions.texte: The movie was ______ boring that I fell asleep.
questions.reponse: so

questions.texte: It was ______ a beautiful day that we decided to go to the beach.
questions.reponse: such

questions.texte: He speaks ______ quickly that nobody can understand him.
questions.reponse: so

questions.texte: They are ______ kind people that everyone loves them.
questions.reponse: such

questions.texte: There was ______ much noise that I couldn't concentrate.
questions.reponse: so

intitule: Sentence Construction with So...that and Such...that

enonce.texte: Combine the two sentences into one using so...that or such...that. The sentence should express a cause and a consequence.

questions.texte: The coffee was hot. I couldn't drink it.
questions.reponse: The coffee was so hot that I couldn't drink it.

questions.texte: She had a big house. Everyone was impressed.
questions.reponse: She had such a big house that everyone was impressed.

questions.texte: He drove fast. He missed the turn.
questions.reponse: He drove so fast that he missed the turn.

questions.texte: There were heavy rains. The streets were flooded.
questions.reponse: There were such heavy rains that the streets were flooded.

questions.texte: The guide gave clear explanations. Everyone understood the lesson.
questions.reponse: The guide gave such clear explanations that everyone understood the lesson.

questions.texte: The child was brave. He went into the dark room alone.
questions.reponse: The child was so brave that he went into the dark room alone.

intitule: Applying So...that and Such...that in Real-Life Contexts

enonce.texte: Read the following real-life scenarios carefully. For each one, create a sentence using so...that or such...that to explain the situation.

questions.texte: A student studied a lot and felt very confident before an exam. Explain the situation.
questions.reponse: The student studied so much that he felt very confident before the exam.

questions.texte: A teacher received a magnificent gift from her students. Explain the situation.
questions.reponse: The students gave the teacher such a magnificent gift that she was deeply moved.

questions.texte: A football team played poorly and lost the match. Explain the situation.
questions.reponse: The team played so poorly that they lost the match.

questions.texte: A flood caused enormous damage; the village was completely destroyed. Explain the situation.
questions.reponse: The flood caused such enormous damage that the village was completely destroyed.

questions.texte: Someone spent a long time in traffic and missed an important meeting.
questions.reponse: There was so much traffic that he/she missed the meeting.

intitule: Analyzing the Impact of Events Using So...that and Such...that

enonce.texte: Read the text about the consequences of bad governance and complete the sentences using so...that or such...that.

questions.texte: In certain African countries, the corruption of leaders is ______ severe that the population lacks even the most basic services.
questions.reponse: so

questions.texte: There is ______ a lack of infrastructure in rural areas that many young people are forced to leave for the cities.
questions.reponse: such

questions.texte: The citizens are ______ angry that they are organizing protests to demand change.
questions.reponse: so

questions.texte: The government has made ______ broken promises that nobody trusts it anymore.
questions.reponse: such

intitule: Transformation: Rephrasing Sentences with So...that and Such...that

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using either so...that or such...that.

questions.texte: The exam was very difficult. Many students failed.
questions.reponse: The exam was so difficult that many students failed.

questions.texte: She told a fascinating story. Everyone listened carefully.
questions.reponse: She told such a fascinating story that everyone listened carefully.

questions.texte: He had a noisy neighbor. He couldn't sleep.
questions.reponse: He had such a noisy neighbor that he couldn't sleep.

questions.texte: They sang beautifully. They received a standing ovation.
questions.reponse: They sang so beautifully that they received a standing ovation.

enonce.texte: Each of the following sentences contains an error in the use of so...that or such...that. Identify the error and correct the sentence.

questions.texte: It was so beautiful weather that we went for a walk.
questions.reponse: It was such beautiful weather that we went for a walk.

sousQuestions.reponse: The word 'weather' is an uncountable noun. 'So' is used before adjectives/adverbs, while 'such' is used before (adjective +) noun. Since 'weather' is a noun, 'such' is required.

questions.texte: He was such tired that he fell asleep immediately.
questions.reponse: He was so tired that he fell asleep immediately.

sousQuestions.reponse: 'Tired' is an adjective. 'So' is used before adjectives/adverbs, while 'such' is used before nouns. Therefore, 'so' is needed.

questions.texte: They have made so progress that the project is almost complete.
questions.reponse: They have made such progress that the project is almost complete.

sousQuestions.reponse: Although 'progress' looks like a verb, it is a noun in this sentence. 'So' is before adjectives/adverbs, so 'such' is necessary here.

questions.texte: She drove so fast car that she lost control of the vehicle.
questions.reponse: She drove such a fast car that she lost control of the vehicle.

sousQuestions.reponse: The structure is incorrect. 'So' is used before an adjective (fast) to modify the verb (drove). To use 'such', we need to consider the noun phrase: 'a fast car'. Therefore, the correct structure is 'such a fast car'.
